D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Smoky Quartz — Al³⁺ Impurity + Natural Radiation

colourless quartz with trace Al³⁺ darkens to brown-grey when ionising radiation knocks an electron loose; the resulting [AlO₄]⁰ centre absorbs broadly across the visible

Pure quartz (SiO₂) is colourless. Substitute one in ~10⁴ Si⁴⁺ with Al³⁺ + charge-compensating H⁺ — still colourless until natural radiation removes one electron from the [AlO₄]⁻ → forming the paramagnetic [AlO₄]⁰ hole centre. Broad visible absorption gives the iconic smoky brown. Heating to 400 °C reverses the colour (electron returns).

Drag the cumulative radiation dose; the crystal darkens. Heating slider resets it. Real Scottish/Brazilian cairngorms accumulate over geological time near uranium-bearing rocks.
